description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of evenly spaced vertical slats connected by two horizontal rails, one at the top and one at the bottom. The slats are rectangular and have a uniform width, with the top edge of the fence forming a flat, straight line. The ends of the horizontal rails extend slightly beyond the outermost slats, giving the fence a framed appearance. The wood has a light, natural finish, suggesting it is either untreated or lightly varnished. There are no visible decorative elements, carvings, or embellishments, and the fence posts do not have any distinct caps or finials. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for use in residential yards, parks, or along sidewalks.
description: A simple wooden picket fence with evenly spaced vertical slats and two horizontal rails, finished in a light natural wood color. The top edge is flat and there are no decorative features or post caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the evenly spaced vertical slats and the flat top edge, with both horizontal rails visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the depth of the rails and slats.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same arrangement of slats and rails visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the rails and slats.
